title: Glorious Yeasty Chinese Noodles
description: A midnight-craving Chinese dish featuring yeasty, aerated noodles, made with water, pickle, and macrobiotic-friendly ingredients, perfect for satisfying late night hunger.

ingredients.name: Chinese noodles
ingredients.notes: preferably made from rice or millet flour

instructions: Combine yeast and warm water in a bowl, stirring to dissolve the yeast, then let it sit for 5 minutes.
Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add thinly sliced scallions and cook for another minute, until they start to soften.
Add Chinese noodles to the skillet, stirring to combine with the ginger and scallions.
Add the yeast mixture, pickle juice, soy sauce, and salt to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, until the noodles are cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the noodles hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
